title: ophrys fusca subsp. marmorata
intro: Ophrys fusca subsp. marmorata, commonly known as the Marbled Bee Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ophrys fusca subsp. marmorata include its ability to treat digestive issues, wounds, and skin problems, making it a valuable herb in traditional folk medicine. In herbal preparations, it is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be dried and used in potpourri blends.
